summary: marked-prettier ships shell-history exfiltration via bash/zsh execSync
details: The package (name suggests a Markdown/Prettier utility) ships lib/collect.js which requires child_process and invokes execSync against bash and zsh to read shell history and related shell state. Shell history routinely contains credentials, tokens, and connection strings; harvesting it via bash/zsh subprocesses is credential collection against the installer regardless of any 'collect'/'logging' framing the package presents. The declared purpose of the package (markdown formatting) does not require reading shell history through bash -c / zsh subprocess invocations. This behavior is inconsistent with a Markdown-related library and matches the shell-history-harvest fingerprint for installer-side data theft.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
